Example 6.5: Ordered versus multinomial logit

Source: vignettes/example-6-5.Rmd
example-6-5.Rmd

These data and specifications reproduce the corresponding example in Mize, Doan, and Long (2019) and on the mecompare documentation page. The code chunks are not evaluated while building the package website because they download the public replication data. Copy and run the code interactively to reproduce the results.

Load and prepare the data 

library(haven)
library(MASS)
library(nnet)
library(marginaleffects)
library(suest)

gss <- zap_labels(read_dta(
  "https://tdmize.github.io/data/data/gss_cme.dta"
))

vars <- c(
  "partyid5", "woman", "edyrs", "age", "parent", "married",
  "faminc", "employed", "region4", "year", "race"
)

dat <- subset(gss, year >= 2010)
dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat[vars]), ]
dat[c("woman", "parent", "married", "race",
      "employed", "region4", "year")] <-
  lapply(dat[c("woman", "parent", "married", "race",
               "employed", "region4", "year")], factor)

party_levels <- sort(unique(dat$partyid5))
dat$party_ord <- ordered(dat$partyid5, levels = party_levels)
dat$party_nom <- factor(dat$partyid5, levels = party_levels)

Fit and combine the models 

ordered_model <- polr(
  party_ord ~ age + I(age^2) + woman + edyrs + parent +
    married + race + faminc + employed + region4 + year,
  data = dat,
  method = "logistic",
  Hess = TRUE
)

nominal_model <- multinom(
  party_nom ~ age + I(age^2) + woman + edyrs + parent +
    married + race + faminc + employed + region4 + year,
  data = dat,
  Hess = TRUE,
  trace = FALSE
)

combined <- suest(
  ordered_model,
  nominal_model,
  model_names = c("Ordered", "Multinomial")
)

Compare category probabilities

For a standard empirical-distribution average, marginaleffects can calculate category-specific changes directly:

avg_comparisons(
  combined,
  variables = list(age = function(x) {
    data.frame(lo = x, hi = x + 10)
  }),
  newdata = dat
)

The published example evaluates a change from age 20 to age 30 with all other model-matrix columns held at their means. The package’s full acceptance suite implements that exact delta-method calculation and reproduces the Stata results:

Category Ordered effect Ordered SE Nominal effect Nominal SE Difference Difference SE
Strong Democrat 0.020 0.004 0.032 0.003 -0.012 0.003
Democrat 0.023 0.005 -0.010 0.011 0.034 0.009
Independent -0.003 0.000 -0.000 0.010 -0.003 0.010
Republican -0.024 0.005 -0.031 0.011 0.007 0.009
Strong Republican -0.016 0.004 0.010 0.003 -0.026 0.004
